summary: Matched two Corneria stage functions in C, primarily grCorneria_801DD674 and grCorneria_801E0A74. The decomp.dev bot reported GALE01 matched code improvement of +1268 bytes (+0.03%), with grCorneria_801DD674 (+812 bytes) and grCorneria_801E0A74 (+456 bytes) reaching 100.00%. The PR also expanded Corneria ground variable layout, tightened grcorneria prototypes, fixed related grmaterial and grzakogenerator signatures, and split/typed several grCn data symbols as strings in symbols.txt.
what_changed: Implemented grCorneria_801DD674 in C, replacing the placeholder. The function initializes many gr->gv.corneria fields, configures animation and materials, toggles JObj hidden flags, creates two Zako generators from grCn_803E1FE8 and grCn_803E2000, registers fn_801DD65C with Ground_801C10B8, and stores Ground_801C3FA4(ground_gobj, 8) into x12C.

Implemented grCorneria_801E0A74(f32*) in C. It checks map kinds 8, 9, and 4 via Ground_801C2BA4, compares the input x coordinate against HSD_JObjGetTranslationX ranges scaled by Ground_801C0498(), returns the matching map kind, and otherwise reports/asserts with grcorneria.c diagnostics.
Corrected fn_801DD65C to clear gv.corneria.xC4_b0 instead of the old x0_b0 name, reflecting the actual offset within Ground variables.

Expanded struct grCorneria_GroundVars from a minimal placeholder with x0_b0/padding/x68 into offset-named fields from xC4_b0 through x12C, including material handles x120/x124, generated object handles xC8/xCC, a created HSD_GObj* x128, and HSD_JObj* x12C.

Updated grcorneria.h prototypes to remove many UNK_PARAMS for simple bool(void) and void(void) functions, and gave matched or newly used functions concrete signatures such as grCorneria_801DD674(Ground_GObj*), grCorneria_801E0A74(f32*), grCorneria_801E0C3C(HSD_GObj*), fn_801E12D4(HSD_GObj*), and grCorneria_801E1878(Ground_GObj*).

Adjusted related API declarations: grMaterial_801C8CFC now takes a void (*)(void) callback parameter in grmaterial.h, and grZakoGenerator_801CA394 was typed as returning int with arguments (UNK_T, int, UNK_T, f32).

Split and retyped several grCn symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt, including changing some data objects to data:string and refining sizes around grCn_803E1F08, grCn_803E1F3C, grCn_803E2000, grCn_803E2124, grCn_803E21A4, grCn_803E21C4, grCn_803E26B4, grCn_803E26C4, grCn_803E26DC, and grCn_804D4650-804D4680.
The grcorneria assembly listing was regenerated/annotated with section ranges and per-function size comments, and several raw .4byte string blobs were rendered as .string objects.
smart_moves: The struct work renamed placeholder fields by their true Ground-relative offsets, e.g. x0_b0 became xC4_b0 and x68 became x12C. This made the matched code use meaningful offset-stable fields without inventing semantic names prematurely.

The grCorneria_801DD674 match reconstructed initialization order carefully: flags, animation setup, position translate, material creation, JObj visibility, enemy generator creation, callback registration, and final x12C storage all appear in one exact setup sequence.

The PR did not just add C bodies; it fixed surrounding type information so the matched code could call existing helpers naturally. Examples include grCorneria_801DD534 returning HSD_GObj*, grCorneria_801E0A74 taking f32*, and grZakoGenerator_801CA394 returning the handle stored in xC8/xCC.

Data-symbol cleanup split large ambiguous blobs into smaller objects and marked obvious literals as data:string, which supports both matching and later readability without requiring full semantic naming.

The grCorneria_801E0A74 implementation reused the same range-check pattern for map kinds 8 and 9 before handling map kind 4 with a wider 4800.0f range, minimizing control-flow risk in a small matching target.
decomp_lessons: For stage ground vars, prefer offset-accurate placeholder field names when semantics are not known. Correcting the union-relative offset names can unblock matching while keeping future renaming safe.

When matching stage initialization functions, fill in the whole local state layout and all helper calls around animation/material/generator setup; these functions often depend on exact call order and flag writes.
Typed headers are part of the match. Updating prototypes away from UNK_PARAMS can be necessary for call signatures, callback passing, return-value use, and stack/register agreement.

Do not leave large data blobs unsplit when a matched function references strings or tables inside them. Refining symbol sizes and marking strings in symbols.txt can make C references and assertions line up cleanly.

Simple accessor/classifier functions like grCorneria_801E0A74 can be good matching wins when their control flow is a repeated null-check plus floating-point bounds test against stage object translations.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names were preserved: grCorneria_801DD674, grCorneria_801E0A74, fn_801DD65C, fn_801E12D0, fn_801E12D4.
Corneria data symbols use the grCn_ address convention, such as grCn_803E1FE8, grCn_803E2000, grCn_803E2124, and grCn_804D4650.
Unknown struct fields in grCorneria_GroundVars were named by their effective offsets, e.g. xC4_b0, xC4_b1, xD4, x120, x124, x128, x12C.

Header cleanup replaced generic UNK_PARAMS with explicit void for no-argument bool/void functions, e.g. bool grCorneria_801DD52C(void), bool grCorneria_801E0D28(void), and bool grCorneria_801E2EE4(void).
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The matched functions removed placeholders for grCorneria_801DD674 and grCorneria_801E0A74 and yielded a bot-confirmed +1268 matched bytes.
grCorneria_801DD674 declares an unused u32 local in the diff; the PR does not explain why, but this kind of retained unused local is often a stack-layout matching aid.
The C uses exact float constants visible in the matched logic: 250.0f, 4.0f, 16.0f, 0.3f, 3200.0f, 4800.0f, 106.0f, and 107.0f.

The PR refined .data and .sdata string objects from raw .4byte data, including strings such as "grcorneria.c", "!(jobj->flags & JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION)", "translate", "grcorneria.c    grCorneriaGetPosMapKind2
", "talk no = %d
", "*** Beam Damage! %f
", and "SIS_GrCorneriaData".
The regenerated asm includes section and per-function size annotations such as '# 0x801DCCFC..0x801E302C | size: 0x6330' and '# .text:... | size: ...', useful for auditing remaining asm coverage.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported: matched code 26.61% (+0.03%, +1268 bytes), with grCorneria_801DD674 and grCorneria_801E0A74 both at 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that give semantic names to grCorneria_GroundVars fields xC4_b0 through x12C.
Search for later matches of grCorneria_801E08CC, grCorneria_801E0C3C, and nearby Corneria map-kind functions.
Check whether grMaterial_801C8DE0 received a proper declaration in grmaterial.h after this PR instead of a local extern in grcorneria.c.
Look for later typing of grZakoGenerator_801CA394 arguments beyond UNK_T.
Compare other stage setup functions that call grMaterial_801C8CFC and grZakoGenerator_801CA394 for reusable initialization patterns.
